card.intuition: A standard way for servers to push data to the browser over a single, long-lived HTTP connection — one-way only.
card.analogy: A radio station: the DJ (server) broadcasts music to your radio (browser) continuously, but you can't talk back through the radio.
when_to_use: Progress bars for long-running server tasks
Social media feeds / notifications where the user doesn't need to respond instantly
Simpler alternative to WebSockets when you only need server-to-client updates
gotchas: Browsers have a limit on the number of open SSE connections per domain (usually 6)

exercise.prompt: Fill in the required HTTP header and data format for SSE.
exercise.starter_code:
# Server response headers
headers = {
    "Content-Type": "text/________-stream",
    "Cache-Control": "no-cache",
    "Connection": "keep-alive"
}

# Message format
message = "____: My notification alert\n\n"
hints: The content type for SSE is 'text/event-stream'
Each message must start with 'data:' followed by the content
